title: Restore CKC book-style visual layout and work-surface behavior
operator_request: CKC visually worked like a book: left panel almost always showed images from a character sheet, right panel was the editable sheet, and only stories/character sheet notes/moodboard created a third middle panel.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Use Argus visual inspection before and after layout changes. Do not reintroduce the four-window grid inside Atelier.

scope.summary: Make CKC feel like the original high-value production app: image-focused left side, editable work surface right side, optional middle panel only for story/notes/moodboard modes.
base_scope: Implement CKC default split: image/media viewer on left, character sheet editor on right.
Add middle-panel mode only for story, character-sheet notes, and moodboard workflows.
Keep image notes separate from character sheet notes in the UI.
Ensure responsive layout remains readable and non-overlapping.
Update internal UserManual with CKC visual workflow.
acceptance_criteria: CKC default view shows character images and editable sheet together.
Story/notes/moodboard modes can expose a third working area without becoming the default.
Argus can inspect tab, viewer, sheet, and middle-panel states.
No unwanted four-window layout appears inside Atelier.
verification: Native UI layout tests for default and middle-panel modes.
Argus screenshots/snapshots for desktop and constrained view sizes.
Manual-content tests for CKC layout guidance.
